content: Two screening approaches: One-step (IADPSG/WHO): 75g OGTT at 24-28 weeks; diagnose if any value meets/exceeds: fasting ≥92 mg/dL, 1-hour ≥180 mg/dL, 2-hour ≥153 mg/dL. Two-step (common in US): 50g glucose challenge test (non-fasting); if 1-hour ≥130-140 mg/dL, proceed to diagnostic 100g OGTT (Carpenter-Coustan criteria): diagnose if ≥2 values meet/exceed: fasting ≥95, 1-hour ≥180, 2-hour ≥155, 3-hour ≥140 mg/dL. Early screening (<24 weeks) recommended for high-risk women using standard diagnostic criteria. Universal screening recommended at 24-28 weeks. Postpartum screening essential: 75g OGTT at 6-12 weeks postpartum, then every 1-3 years given high progression risk to T2DM.
